summary: Package fetches executable payload from anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket
details: dist/bootstrap.js fetches content from https://pub-046c52795b9445cd9f5cc5cb21b9d59f.r2.dev — an anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket of the same shape used by previously-confirmed payload-distribution infrastructure. The fetch occurs alongside reads of process.env, and the bootstrap module is reached at startup. Anonymous R2 buckets are mutable and have no publisher accountability; legitimate vendor SDKs do not deliver code from pub-<hex>.r2.dev hosts. The second flagged file (dist/api/dadosabertos.js) calls https://apidadosabertos.saude.gov.br, which is the package's documented Brazilian public-health-data API and is consistent with the package's stated purpose — that endpoint is not the concern. The R2 bucket fetch in bootstrap.js is.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
